prose:
Frontier-orbitals task on O2 (triplet). Method: DFT, tier 'standard' = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fit RI on by default), spin-unrestricted UKS (open-shell), in PySCF. Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation). Charge 0, multiplicity 3 as specified. Geometry: user-supplied oxygen.xyz (2 atoms, O-O); single point at the supplied geometry, NOT re-optimized.

Headline result: HOMO-LUMO gap = 5.2880586 eV (Koopmans-style orbital-energy difference, not an excitation energy). Supporting values: HOMO (index 15) = -8.8739616 eV (occ 1.0, degenerate with HOMO-1 at the same energy); LUMO (index 16) = -3.5859031 eV (occ 0.0, degenerate with LUMO+1 at the same energy). Total electronic energy = -150.38872 Hartree (-4092.2855 eV; energy zero = bare nuclei + electrons, comparable only to same-method/same-basis values). Koopmans descriptors: vertical IP = 8.8739616 eV, vertical EA = 3.5859031 eV, electronegativity = 6.2299323 eV, chemical hardness = 2.6440293 eV.

SCF converged in 7 cycles. Engine integrity: status 'ok', trustworthy = true (energy finite, SCF converged, schema header/headline present). The result JSON contained NO warnings field / no warnings reported (clean run). No experimental comparison is offered.

Reproducibility: cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 3 <oxygen.xyz>; a live .out log is streamed by the engine to its run directory during the run.
